analysis.core_story_summary: [From the 7th chapter of the Pratisarga Parva. ] The sun god, being pleased by the praise of the demigods, said: By the order of the Lord Krishna, Sudarshana will take birth in the Dvapara-yuga, and will be known as Nimbaditya (Nimbarka). He will be able to remove the declination of religious principles.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: References family and marriage roles rooted in traditional contexts.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **THE PREDICTION OF NIMBARKA ACHARYA — Kids (8-12):** [From the 7th chapter of the Pratisarga Parva. ] The sun god, being pleased by the praise of the demigods, said: By the order of the Lord Krishna, Sudarshana will take birth in the Dvapara-yuga, and will be known as Nimbaditya (Nimbarka). He will be able to remove the declination of religious principles. This story teaches respect for nature. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **THE PREDICTION OF NIMBARKA ACHARYA — Teens (13-17):** [From the 7th chapter of the Pratisarga Parva. ] The sun god, being pleased by the praise of the demigods, said: By the order of the Lord Krishna, Sudarshana will take birth in the Dvapara-yuga, and will be known as Nimbaditya (Nimbarka). He will be able to remove the declination of religious principles. For teens, the key idea is respect for nature. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.

He will be able to remove the declination of religious principles. A modern takeaway is respect for nature. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.

He will be able to remove the declination of religious principles. The episode highlights respect for nature. redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **THE PREDICTION OF NIMBARKA ACHARYA — Global General Audience:** [From the 7th chapter of the Pratisarga Parva. ] The sun god, being pleased by the praise of the demigods, said: By the order of the Lord Krishna, Sudarshana will take birth in the Dvapara-yuga, and will be known as Nimbaditya (Nimbarka). He will be able to remove the declination of religious principles. In universal terms, it explores respect for nature. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
